commit.message:
Fix symmetric triangle waveform: slow EMA alpha 0.05 -> 0.005

With alpha=0.05 the DC baseline had a ~0.4s time constant — shorter than
the ~0.87s beat interval at 69 BPM — so the EMA was tracking the pulse
itself, producing a symmetric up/down triangle with no gaps.

Changing to alpha=0.005 gives a ~4s time constant. The baseline now tracks
only slow drift, leaving the true PPG pulse shape (fast rise, slower fall
with dicrotic notch, flat gap between beats) visible in the AC signal.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix startup waveform swing: add calibration warmup phase

The EMA baseline was initializing from a low value during finger placement
then slowly climbing to the real level (~250k), causing a large transient
swing that pushed the line to the bottom before settling.

Fix:
- Separate DC update from waveform drawing
- During warmup (60 samples, ~1.2s): use fast alpha (0.5) to snap the
  baseline to the correct level in ~6 samples; show 'Hold still...' with
  a progress bar instead of drawing the waveform
- After warmup: switch to slow alpha (0.05) for gentle drift tracking;
  draw waveform normally with AC-only signal

commit.message:
fix: reliably read session in tRPC context using next/headers cookies()

Root cause: auth() uses createActionURL() which, without AUTH_URL set, falls
back to x-forwarded-proto header. In local dev this header is absent, so it
defaults to 'https' → useSecureCookies=true → looks for '__Secure-authjs.session-token'
cookie. But the login set an unscoped 'authjs.session-token' cookie (HTTP).
Cookie name/salt mismatch → JWE decryption fails → session null → UNAUTHORIZED.

Fix:
- Use cookies() from next/headers in createContext — guaranteed correct in
  Next.js route handlers regardless of request headers
- Try both cookie names (HTTP and HTTPS) so the same code works in dev and prod
- Add AUTH_URL=http://localhost:3000 to .env.local as the canonical base URL

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update routes/auth.js

commit.message:
Fix waveform stuck at top: correct auto-scale initial values

irMin was initialized to 200000 but real finger IR readings are often
245000+, so irMin never got updated and the artificial floor compressed
all real signal variation to the very top of the display.

Initialize irMin=300000 (will drop on first reading) and irMax=0
(will rise on first reading) so auto-scaling tracks the actual signal.

commit.message:
fix(eval_log_stripper): handle NaN/Infinity in eval logs with streaming preservation (#966)

## Overview

Fixes `IncompleteJSONError` when parsing eval logs containing
`NaN`/`Infinity` values. Preserves the original values in the
`.fast.eval` output.

**Issue:** [HAWK-3PV](https://metr-sh.sentry.io/issues/HAWK-3PV)

## Approach and Alternatives

Two streaming file filters bracket the existing transform in
`_transform_sample_entry`:

1. **Forward filter** (`sanitize_nan_to_file`): Streams byte-by-byte
tracking JSON string context, replaces `NaN`/`Infinity`/`-Infinity` with
sentinel strings (`"__HAWK_NAN__"` etc.) so ijson sees valid JSON
2. **Reverse filter** (`restore_nan_from_file`): Simple streaming
`bytes.replace` restoring sentinels back to original literals

This preserves the original values end-to-end while keeping the
streaming architecture (individual samples can be 30GB+).

| Approach | Pros | Cons |
|----------|------|------|
| **Sentinel round-trip (chosen)** | Preserves values, streaming,
correct (string-context aware) | Two extra I/O passes per sample |
| Regex replace to null | Simpler | Lossy — NaN becomes null |
| simplejson with allow_nan | Native support | Not streaming, defeats
memory efficiency |
| In-memory stream wrapper | Single pass | Complex read() protocol with
chunk buffering |

## Testing & Validation

- [x] Covered by automated tests

## Checklist
- [x] Code follows the project's style guidelines
- [x] Self-review completed (especially for LLM-written code)
- [x] Comments added for complex or non-obvious code
- [x] Uninformative LLM-generated comments removed
- [x] Tests added or updated (if applicable)

## Additional Context

Alternative to #964 which does the replacement in-memory.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Add CI quality gate to block deployment on test/lint failure (#97)

Deployment had no quality gate — code could be deployed to Azure Static
Web Apps regardless of test or lint results.

## Workflow changes

- **New `test_and_quality` job** runs before deployment: installs root +
API deps, runs `npm run lint` and `npm test`
- **`build_and_deploy_job` gated via `needs: test_and_quality`** —
deployment is automatically skipped when any check fails
- Fixed `actions/checkout` from nonexistent `v6` → `v4`
- Added explicit `permissions` blocks to both jobs (principle of least
privilege)

```yaml
jobs:
  test_and_quality:
    permissions:
      contents: read
    steps:
      - run: npm ci && cd api && npm ci
      - run: npm run lint
      - run: npm test

  build_and_deploy_job:
    needs: test_and_quality   # ← deployment blocked if gate fails
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write
      statuses: write
```

> **Note:** `cd api && npm ci` is required — the root `npm ci` omits
Azure SDK deps needed by `api/tests/services/`, causing 2 suites to fail
without it.

## Docs

Added `documentation/ci-quality-gate.md`: gate behaviour, local
reproduction steps, and how to fix lint/test failures.

commit.message:
fix: 修正置闰判断 - 看符头到大雪/芒种的距离

置闰规则：符头到大雪（或芒种）的距离超过9天时置闰
- 往前遍历找到最近的大雪或芒种节气
- 计算符头到该节气的天数
- 超过9天则置闰，继续使用大雪/芒种的局

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: set HOME=/data in Dockerfile so Copilot/Codex CLI can write their cache

When the container runs as a custom UID (e.g. Synology NAS `user: "1026:100"`),
the default HOME is `/` which is not writable. Copilot CLI writes to $HOME/.copilot
and fails with EACCES. Setting HOME=/data points it to the already-mounted,
writable data volume.
